summary: Build GameCommand validation/application plus DomainEvent, StateDelta, and Query v1
acceptance_criteria: GameCommand v1 is a versioned discriminated union with only minimal M1 kernel commands needed for boot, deterministic advancement, fixture/debug commands, and hash verification.
Player and AI callers use the same command type, validator, apply path, and result shape; no player-only or AI-only authority mutation path exists.
previewCommand validates without mutating state; applyCommand re-validates at commit time and atomically accepts or rejects with stable reason codes.
Expected command failures use structured CommandRejected/DomainError outcomes, not exceptions or text matching.
Committed changes emit a clear DomainEvent and/or StateDelta boundary suitable for read models; UI, Pixi, Electron, and Node IO do not receive mutable WorldState authority.
Minimal Query v1 exposes read-model-safe data and state hash queries without letting UI traverse or mutate WorldState internals.
Node runner and Web Worker path share the same sim-core boot/preview/submit/query/advance implementation; duplicated authority logic in adapters is forbidden.
Same seed, initial state, command sequence, and scheduler order produce the same state hash in Node runner and Worker path.
required_tests: Command tests cover valid, invalid, duplicate/replayed, stale-day, bad-ID, and boundary payload cases.
Contract tests cover Node runner and Worker boot/submit/preview/query producing identical hashes for the same command sequence.
Protocol/schema tests reject unknown message types and malformed command/query payloads with serializable errors.
notes: ADR-004 already decides explicit GameCommand, semantic Domain Events/ReadModelDelta, and snapshot plus short tail; do not expand to full event sourcing.
If command/protocol semantics exceed ADR-004, write a new ADR before implementation.
